logs.content: **Hello! 👋**, I'm **Agent Zero**, your AI assistant. How can I help you today?

## Role You are Michael's executive assistant. Your job is to produce a comprehensive daily briefing by collecting pre-summarized data from multiple sources, then applying your intelligence to identify what matters, what needs action, and what can wait. ## Goal Produce a structured daily briefing synthesizing all sources into a holistic work report with four sections: a delivery health snapshot, action items pending Michael's response, follow-ups where Michael is waiting on others, work stream summaries, and patterns/observations. Every item must be detailed enough that Michael can act on it without opening any links. --- ## Schedule - **Monday:** Use `--days 5` (covers the weekend) - **Tuesday - Friday:** Use `--days 2` - **Saturday & Sunday:** Skip - no briefing on weekends Determine today's day of the week and set `DAYS` accordingly before running Phase 1. --- ## Phase 1: Data Collection Run all skill scripts **sequentially** using subordinate `assistant` agents - one agent per skill. Each skill uses SQLite caching with incremental fetching. ⚠️ **CRITICAL: Each skill interacts with live browser sessions and APIs. Expect 5-15 minutes per skill. Each subordinate agent MUST wait for the script to print `ALL DONE` before responding back.** Do NOT timeout, skip, or move on early. ### Step 0: Internal Context (do this yourself - no subordinate needed) Search memory for action items, follow-ups, pending tasks, and commitments using these keywords: "action item", "follow-up", "TODO", "pending", "waiting on", "committed to", "promised", "deadline". Then read files matching `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report *.md` from the last `DAYS` days and extract unresolved action items and follow-ups. Hold this data in memory (it is small). ### Step 1: Jira (via subordinate agent) Spawn a subordinate `assistant` agent with this exact task: > Run the following command in a bash terminal and wait until you see `[jira_reader] ALL DONE` printed to stdout. Do NOT stop monitoring early. Once you see `ALL DONE`, respond with only: "Jira complete. Output file: /a0/usr/skills/jira/data/jira-output.md" > > Command: > ```bash > python /a0/usr/skills/jira/scripts/jira_reader.py --filter-id 13811 --view-id 10489904 > ``` > Monitor progress via: `/a0/usr/skills/jira/data/jira-debug.log` > The script will print `[Start fetching X/N]` and `[Completed fetching X/N]` per item, and a final `ALL DONE` banner when fully complete. Wait for the subordinate agent to respond with the output file path before proceeding to Step 2. ### Step 2: Google Chat (via subordinate agent) Spawn a subordinate `assistant` agent with this exact task: > Run the following command in a bash terminal and wait until you see `[gchat_reader] ALL DONE` printed to stdout. Do NOT stop monitoring early. Once you see `ALL DONE`, respond with only: "GChat complete. Output file: /a0/usr/skills/gchat/data/gchat-output.md" > > Command: > ```bash > python /a0/usr/skills/gchat/scripts/gchat_reader.py --days $DAYS > ``` > Monitor progress via: `/a0/usr/skills/gchat/data/gchat-debug.log` > The script will print `[Start fetching X/N]` and `[Completed fetching X/N]` per conversation, and a final `ALL DONE` banner when fully complete. Wait for the subordinate agent to respond with the output file path before proceeding to Step 3. ### Step 3: Gmail (via subordinate agent) Spawn a subordinate `assistant` agent with this exact task: > Run the following command in a bash terminal and wait until you see `[gmail_reader] ALL DONE` printed to stdout. Do NOT stop monitoring early. Once you see `ALL DONE`, respond with only: "Gmail complete. Output file: /a0/usr/skills/gmail/data/gmail-output.md" > > Command: > ```bash > python /a0/usr/skills/gmail/scripts/gmail_reader.py --days $DAYS > ``` > Monitor progress via: `/a0/usr/skills/gmail/data/gmail-debug.log` > The script will print `[Start fetching X/N]` and `[Completed fetching X/N]` per thread, and a final `ALL DONE` banner when fully complete. Wait for the subordinate agent to respond with the output file path before proceeding to Phase 2. --- ## Phase 2: Read Output Files ⚠️ **Read the output files ONLY NOW, immediately before generating the report.** Do NOT read them earlier - this avoids prematurely enlarging the context window. Read the following three files and hold their content for analysis: 1. `/a0/usr/skills/jira/data/jira-output.md` 2. `/a0/usr/skills/gchat/data/gchat-output.md` 3. `/a0/usr/skills/gmail/data/gmail-output.md` --- ## Skill Output Format Each output file contains pre-summarized blocks. Each block follows this structure: ``` ## {source}/{resource_id}: {title} Source: {source} | Key/Thread/Group: {resource_id} | {additional metadata} [AI-generated summary - current status, decisions, pending actions, key dates, blockers] ``` Where `source` is `jira`, `gmail`, or `gchat`. The summaries are already AI-generated and condensed - your job is to triage and organize them, not re-summarize. --- ## Phase 3: Intelligent Analysis and Synthesis You have pre-summarized data from Jira, GChat, and Gmail. Follow the exact steps below to produce the daily work report. Do NOT skip steps. Do NOT invent data. Do NOT produce vague summaries. Refer to the example report at `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report.example.md` to understand the expected quality, depth, and formatting. --- ### Step 1: Pre-Analysis Pass (do this in your head before writing anything) Read ALL three output files completely, then do the following mentally: **A. Group items into work streams.** A "work stream" is a named project, initiative, or recurring process - NOT a ticket number or a chat space name. One work stream may appear across multiple sources (e.g., "BCRS Phase 2" may have Jira tickets, GChat threads, and emails all talking about it). Group all related items under one work stream name. **B. Separate signal from noise.** Most items are informational updates requiring no action. Only a small subset require Michael to DO something or FOLLOW UP. Be ruthless - if an item is just an FYI, it belongs in Work Streams or Patterns, not in Action Items. **C. Identify cross-source items.** If the same topic appears in 2 or 3 sources (e.g., Jira + GChat + Gmail), that item is more important. Note this in your output. **D. Check for implied actions.** Some items look informational but imply action. Examples: - A question directed at Michael with no answer yet = Action Item - A ticket blocked because Michael hasn't signed off = Action Item - A team member waiting on Michael's input = Action Item - A ticket Michael owns that is stalled = Follow-Up (waiting on someone else) - An alert that auto-resolved with no human needed = informational only, put in Work Streams **E. Assign urgency using these rules:** - 🔴 Critical = deadline today or tomorrow, OR blocking someone else's work right now, OR executive-level request - 🟡 Important = SLA risk this week, manager request, or team-level blocker - 🟢 Nice-to-have = no immediate deadline, can be batched, purely informational --- ### Step 2: Write the Executive Summary Write 4-6 sentences of plain prose (NO tables, NO bullet points, NO headings). This is the first thing Michael reads - it must give him the full picture in under 30 seconds. Cover ALL of the following in your sentences: 1. What are the 2-3 most important things happening today in Michael's work? 2. What is the current delivery state - what is on track, what is at risk, and what is blocked? 3. Are there any notable people dynamics, team wins, or concerns? Rules: - Write it LAST (after you have drafted all other sections and have the full picture) but place it FIRST in the report, right after the title and `---` separator, before any section header - Do NOT add a heading like "Executive Summary" or "Overview" - just start the prose directly - Do NOT add "Single most important focus" or any call-out box - end the prose naturally after covering the 3 points above --- ### Step 3: Write Section 1 - My Action Items **What belongs here:** Items where Michael must personally do something - reply, approve, deploy, provide info, attend, or decide. **What does NOT belong here:** Items where Michael is just monitoring, or where someone else owns the work. For each action item, write one table row in EXACTLY this format: ``` | Metadata | Details | Suggested Action | |----------|---------|-----------------| | **Priority:** 🔴/🟡/🟢
**Priority reason:** [1 sentence - why is this urgent or important?]
**Source:** [Jira / GChat / Gmail / Memory - be specific, e.g., "GChat (BCRS Firefighting Group)"]
**Date:** [When was this requested or when is it due?]
**From:** [Full name of the person who asked or created this] | **Context:** [3-5 sentences: (1) What is the situation? (2) What exactly is Michael asked to do? (3) What happens if Michael does not act? (4) Any deadline or dependency.]
**References:** [Exact ticket keys like DPD-715, or thread names, or resource_ids from the output files]
**Cross-source signals:** [List other sources where this topic appears, e.g., "GChat (3 threads), Jira (2 tickets)". If only one source, write "Single source."] | [1-2 sentences. Start with a verb. Name the person and the channel. E.g., "DM Nikhil Grover on GChat to confirm split flag config, then approve DPD-715 and DPD-733 UAT in Jira."] | ``` Sort rows by priority: all 🔴 first, then all 🟡, then all 🟢. --- ### Step 4: Write Section 2 - My Follow-Ups **What belongs here:** Items where Michael has already asked for something and is waiting for someone else to respond or deliver. Michael has done his part; the ball is in someone else's court. For each follow-up, write one table row in EXACTLY this format: ``` | Metadata | Details | Suggested Action | |----------|---------|-----------------| | **Priority:** 🔴/🟡/🟢
**Priority reason:** [1 sentence]
**Source:** [Jira / GChat / Gmail - be specific]
**Since:** [Date Michael last asked or sent the request]
**Days Waiting:** [Calculate: today's date minus the "Since" date]
**Waiting On:** [Full name + their role/team] | **Context:** [3-4 sentences: (1) What is pending? (2) Why does it matter? (3) What breaks if this stays unresolved?]
**References:** [Exact ticket keys or thread names]
**Cross-source signals:** [Other sources or "Single source."] | [Based on days waiting: 0-1 day = "Give it until tomorrow"; 2-3 days = "Send a gentle GChat nudge today"; 4-7 days = "Direct message [name] today and set a response deadline of [date]"; 7+ days = "Escalate to [name's manager] if no reply by [date]."] | ``` Sort rows by priority: all 🔴 first, then 🟡, then 🟢. --- ### Step 5: Write Section 3 - Work Stream Summaries **What belongs here:** Everything that did NOT become an action item or follow-up, grouped by the work streams you identified in Step 1. Also include context for action items and follow-ups (e.g., the full BCRS situation, not just the specific action). For each work stream, write this block: ``` ### [Work Stream Name] - **[Status: On Track / At Risk / Blocked / Completed / Paused]** - **What's happening:** [3-5 sentences: What is the current state? What progress happened recently? What decisions were made? What is pending?] - **Key people:** [List named individuals and their role in this stream. E.g., "Prajney Sribhashyam (coordinator), Michael Bui (deployment owner)"] - **Open questions or risks:** [List unresolved items that don't need Michael's action YET but could escalate. Number them: (1), (2), (3).] - **Recommendation:** [One sentence: Should Michael monitor, deprioritize, step in, or escalate? Be specific.] ``` Order: Most active or highest-risk work streams first. Paused or stable ones last. Skip any work stream with zero activity in the last `DAYS` days. --- ### Step 6: Write Section 4 - Patterns and Observations Write 3-5 bullet points. Each bullet must: - Span at least 2 work streams or 2 sources (this is a cross-cutting observation, not a per-item note) - Be specific - name the people, tickets, or patterns involved - Sound like a trusted advisor's private notes, not a corporate status update Good examples of patterns: - A person or team that consistently delays responses across multiple threads - A risk that is affecting multiple work streams simultaneously - A team win worth acknowledging - A process gap that keeps causing the same problem - A coverage gap because someone is on leave Bad examples (too vague, do NOT write these): - "Communication could be improved" - "Some tickets are delayed" - "The team is busy" --- ### Step 7: Final Assembly Put the sections together in this order: ``` # ❗ Daily Work Report - [Day of week], [Month DD, YYYY] --- [Executive summary prose - 4-6 sentences, no heading, no bullet points] --- ## 🔴 My Action Items (Pending My Action) [Section 1 table] --- ## 🟡 My Follow-Ups (Waiting on Others) [Section 2 table] --- ## 📋 Work Stream Summaries [Section 3 blocks] --- ## 💡 Patterns and Observations [Section 4 bullets] --- **Quick Stats:** [X] action items | [Y] follow-ups | [Z] work streams | [W] patterns/observations | Sources: [n_memory] memory items, [n_jira] Jira, [n_gchat] chats, [n_gmail] emails ``` Rules: - Use EXACTLY the section headers and emoji shown above - Never use vague language like "investigate issue" or "look into this" - always name WHO does WHAT - If a section has no items, write: "None identified in the reporting period." - Count items accurately for Quick Stats (count rows in tables, count work stream blocks, count bullet points) --- ## Save Report After generating the report, save it as: - **Path:** `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report {YYYY-MM-DD}.md` - **Overwrite** if the file already exists - Include all sections, tables, and suggestions exactly as generated - Reference: `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report.example.md` is a complete example of the expected output format and quality ## Error Handling - If any skill script fails, report the error clearly and continue with remaining sources - If env vars are missing (JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_KEY, API_KEY_OTHER), report and skip that source - If Chrome CDP connection fails (gchat/gmail), report and skip those sources - Never silently skip data - always report what succeeded and what failed

logs.heading: A0: Starting daily briefing data collection (Friday: --days 2)

logs.heading: A0: Phase 3: Intelligent analysis and synthesis of all data sources
logs.content:
{ "thoughts": [ "All three data sources have been collected and read: - Jira: 6 tickets (OMNI-1191 blocked, DPD-715/733 done/in release queue, DPD-734 to do, DPD-644/645 done, OPCO-1940 on hold) - GChat: 4 alert spaces (#dd-dpd-engage-alert, Shopping Cart Notification, #dd-dpd-grocery-alert, BCRS-UAT) with critical system instability - Gmail: 7 threads (calendar no events, Axelera AI promo, RMN PRD from Nikhil, Advertima TTD success, BCRS War Room meeting, Project Light meetings) Now I need to perform Phase 3 Intelligent Analysis: 1. Group items into work streams (BCRS/OMNI/RMN/Advertima/Dynamics/Grocery/Personal) 2. Separate signal from noise (identify actual action items vs informational updates) 3. Identify cross-source correlations 4. Check for implied actions and assign urgency 5. Write executive summary, then all 4 sections 6. Save report to workdir", "Work Streams identified: - BCRS/OMNI: OMNI-1191 (Meta blocked), OPCO-1940 (VIP logic on hold), DPD-710 epic tickets - Dynamics/Grocery: System flickering alerts, fp-search-indexer P2 error - RMN/RMN PRD: Nikhil's integration planning email - Advertima/TTD: Lab test success, strategic pivot discussion - Personal Meetings: BCRS War Room (already passed), Project Light (accepted) Priority Assessment: - 🔴 Critical: fp-search-indexer P2 error (active since Mar 18), Shopping Cart Error Budget 94.8% consumed, OMNI-1191 blocked (Revenue at risk) - 🟡 Important: DPD-715/733 UAT complete, Omni Home discrepancy, OPCO-1940 business alignment needed - 🟢 Nice-to-have: Axelera AI contest, RMN PRD review, Advertima strategic discussion" ], "headline": "Phase 3: Intelligent analysis and synthesis of all data sources", "tool_name": "response", "tool_args": { "text": "**Executive Summary** Today's work is dominated by **two critical production incidents**: the `fp-search-indexer` service has been in a P2 error state since March 18 requiring immediate investigation, and the Shopping Cart notification channel reports 94.8% of the 7-day error budget consumed for wishlist operations—both demand urgent root cause analysis. On the delivery front, **OMNI-1191 (Meta offsite ads integration) remains blocked** pending Meta's campaign whitelisting response, putting $600k RMN revenue at risk with a 4-6 week development cycle looming if unblocked this quarter. Meanwhile, the BCRS UAT sign-off is delayed due to Finance and Pre-order test failures (15/15 cases failed), requiring S&G team intervention before any release can proceed. **My Action Items:** | Metadata | Details | Suggested Action | |----------|---------|-----------------| | **Priority:** 🔴
**Priority reason:** P2 error budget at 94.8%—system approaching SLO breach threshold
**Source:** GChat (Shopping Cart Notification)
**Date:** March 19, 2026, 21:29 UTC
**From:** Datadog Automated Alert (`@hangouts-ShoppingCartNotification`) | **Context:** The `frontend-gateway` service consumed 94.845% of its 7-day error budget for `put_product_id_wish_list` at 21:29 UTC on March 19. Multiple latency spikes and success rate drops (<99.9%) persist across read/write paths (Wishlist, Checkout). This is a recurring pattern since March 16 with no sustained resolution. Immediate root cause analysis is required to halt further budget burn before breaching the SLO.
**References:** Event ID `8551227452052743186`, Monitors `21245714`/`21245734`
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **DM the dpd-pricing-cart on-call lead immediately, request incident channel creation, and demand correlation analysis between wishlist latency spikes and checkout failures by 9 AM SGT tomorrow.** | | **Priority:** 🔴
**Priority reason:** Active P2 error state since March 18; zero tolerance policy enforced
**Source:** GChat (#dd-dpd-grocery-alert)
**Date:** March 19, 2026, 15:35 UTC (re-triggered)
**From:** Datadog App (`@oncall-dpd-staff-excellence-pdm`) | **Context:** The `fp-search-indexer` service has been in a P2 error state continuously from March 18 through March 19, 15:35 UTC. The metric `trace.http.request.errors` = 1.0 over the last minute indicates active production failures. Previous "resolved" status is invalid; manual intervention is mandatory. This affects grocery discovery and search indexing reliability.
**References:** Monitor ID `17447691`, APM Trace, K8s Console link, Runbook SR-2001831558
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Page the DPD Grocery Discovery on-call immediately, escalate to Product Data Management if no response within 30 minutes, and initiate runbook SR-2001831558 investigation.** | | **Priority:** 🔴
**Priority reason:** Revenue at risk ($600k RMN Offsite); external blocker pending Meta response
**Source:** Jira (OMNI-1191)
**Date:** Oct 2025–Jan 29, 2026 (blocked >3 months)
**From:** Nikhil Grover (Reporter/Owner) | **Context:** OMNI-1191 (Enable offsite ads integration with Meta on OSMOS) remains blocked because Meta has not whitelisted specific campaigns required for API testing completion. Configuration was enabled Oct 2025, but whitelisting delayed until now. Testing stalled Nov 2025; Meta asked to revert error by end of week Jan 22 with no response. A 4-6 week development cycle is required post-blocker removal, risking missing the 2025 delivery window.
**References:** OMNI-1191, RM-556
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Escalate to Meta senior leadership via existing channel; send Nikhil Grover a direct message demanding Meta escalation log and proposed timeline for whitelisting resolution.** | | **Priority:** 🟡
**Priority reason:** UAT complete but Omni Home discrepancy unresolved; release blocked
**Source:** Jira (DPD-715, DPD-733)
**Date:** March 19, 2026 (UAT completed)
**From:** Nikhil Grover (Reporter) | **Context:** Both DPD-715 and DPD-733 are marked Done but remain in "In Release Queue." UAT was successfully conducted on March 19 by Michael Bui. However, Omni Home swimlanes are stuck at fixed positions (1, 3) instead of reflecting SplitIO dynamic changes. This discrepancy must be clarified with stakeholders before final sign-off and production deployment can proceed.
**References:** DPD-715, DPD-733, Parent DPD-710
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Contact Nikhil Grover to confirm Omni Home stakeholder list, then investigate why SplitIO changes are not reflecting in Omni swimlanes and document findings before next release cycle.** | | **Priority:** 🟡
**Priority reason:** Business alignment required before VIP bypass feature can be released
**Source:** Jira (OPCO-1940)
**Date:** March 16, 2026 (last update)
**From:** Vivian Lim Yu Qian / Ravi Goel | **Context:** OPCO-1940 (FP VIPs encounter verification bypass) is technically Done and UAT signed off on Feb 2, but deployment is paused due to required re-alignment on VIP identification logic. Original implementation used Split flag + external sheet; business now requires direct backoffice tag reference. Datadoghq monitoring approval granted pending resolution.
**References:** OPCO-1940
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Schedule 30-minute alignment call with Business/CS team to confirm VIP tag definition, then document decision and update UAT sign-off accordingly.** | | **Priority:** 🟢
**Priority reason:** Informational; optional participation in community contest
**Source:** Gmail (Axelera AI)
**Date:** March 19, 2026
**From:** Axelera AI Team | **Context:** Axelera AI announced winners of "Smarter Spaces" challenge and launched a new voluntary "Demo Jam" contest. Submissions due April 12, 2026. Prize is a 4 Quad-Core Metis PCIe Card. Includes technical build guide for CCTV integration with Home Assistant.
**References:** Thread ID `19d06d47e91983ee`, community.axelera.ai
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Review at leisure; no immediate action required. Consider participating if interested in Metis/Voyager SDK showcase.** | **My Follow-Ups (Waiting on Others):** | Metadata | Details | Suggested Action | |----------|---------|-----------------| | **Priority:** 🔴
**Priority reason:** Meta response delayed >3 months; revenue at risk
**Source:** Jira (OMNI-1191)
**Since:** Late October 2025
**Days Waiting:** ~98 days
**Waiting On:** Nikhil Grover (following up with Meta) | **Context:** OMNI-1191 has been blocked waiting for Meta's campaign whitelisting since late Oct 2025. Testing resumed Nov 2025 but stalled; Meta asked to revert error by end of week Jan 22, 2026 with no response as of Jan 29. This is a critical external dependency blocking $600k RMN revenue opportunity.
**References:** OMNI-1191
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Escalate to Meta senior leadership via existing channel; set hard deadline for response by March 25, 2026 or initiate alternative vendor engagement.** | | **Priority:** 🟡
**Priority reason:** BCRS UAT sign-off delayed due to unresolved test failures
**Source:** GChat (BCRS-UAT)
**Since:** March 19, 2026
**Days Waiting:** 1 day
**Waiting On:** S&G Team / Testing Team | **Context:** OG Returns & Refunds UAT sign-off was planned for March 19 but delayed. 2 E2E test cases need retesting due to wrong amount posting (fixed, pending retest). In-store Pre-order: all 15 test cases failed because BCRS FOC not charged by POS. Finance Testing: 9/18 cases failed.
**References:** Space AAQACfHCuNI
**Cross-source signals:** Single source. | **Send gentle GChat nudge to Sathya Murthy Karthik today requesting retest schedule and POS charging fix ETA.** | **Work Stream Summaries:** ### BCRS/OMNI - [Status: At Risk] - **What's happening:** Two parallel critical paths. OMNI-1191 remains blocked by Meta's campaign whitelisting (3+ months), threatening $600k RMN revenue. OPCO-1940 VIP bypass feature is technically complete but paused for business logic re-alignment. BCRS UAT sign-off delayed due to Finance and Pre-order test failures. - **Key people:** Nikhil Grover (OMNI owner), Vivian Lim Yu Qian, Ravi Goel, Sathya Murthy Karthik (UAT lead), S&G Team - **Open questions or risks:** (1) Meta whitelisting timeline unknown; (2) VIP tag definition alignment pending; (3) POS charging issue root cause for Pre-order failures unclear. - **Recommendation:** Escalate OMNI blocker immediately; schedule VIP alignment call this week; follow up on BCRS retest schedule daily until resolved. ### Dynamics/Grocery - [Status: Blocked] - **What's happening:** Two critical production incidents. `fp-search-indexer` in P2 error state since March 18 affecting grocery discovery. Shopping Cart Notification channel reports 94.8% error budget consumed for wishlist operations with recurring latency spikes and success rate drops.
- **Key people:** DPD Grocery Discovery On-call, dpd-pricing-cart team, Product Data Management On-call - **Open questions or risks:** (1) Root cause of fp-search-indexer errors unknown; (2) Wishlist latency correlation with checkout failures not established; (3) Error budget breach imminent if not resolved within 24 hours. - **Recommendation:** Treat as P0 incident; page on-calls immediately; initiate runbook investigations; escalate to VP if no progress by EOD tomorrow. ### RMN/RMN PRD - [Status: On Track] - **What's happening:** Nikhil Grover shared RMN PRD and requested integration planning meeting. Meeting invite pending from sender. No immediate action required beyond reviewing document before scheduled session.
- **Key people:** Nikhil Grover, Shubhangi, Rahul - **Open questions or risks:** None identified; standard integration planning workflow. - **Recommendation:** Review PRD at leisure; confirm attendance when meeting invite received. ### Advertima/TTD - [Status: Completed] - **What's happening:** Lab tests officially completed and validated by all stakeholders (Yian Koh, Ravi Singh, Yasmina Tregan). Transition to live operations active. Strategic pivot discussed: next phase integrates visual-spatial intelligence for deterministic shopper IDs with probabilistic segments.
- **Key people:** Iman Nahvi (Advertima), Yian Koh (TTD), Ravi Singh - **Open questions or risks:** None identified; test completion milestone achieved. - **Recommendation:** Monitor live campaigns as they go "live"; stand by for implementation changes if needed. ### Personal Meetings - [Status: Completed/Passed] - **What's happening:** BCRS Refunds War Room meeting occurred March 19, 3:30–4:30 PM (already passed). Project Light meeting accepted for March 19, 4–5 PM (also passed). No upcoming meetings identified in reporting period.
- **Key people:** Prajney Sribhashyam, Alvin Choo - **Open questions or risks:** None. - **Recommendation:** Review meeting notes if available; no action required. **Patterns and Observations:** - ⚠️ **Systemic instability pattern:** `frontend-gateway` (Orchid latency P99 spikes to ~20s) and `engage-my-persona-api-go` (MyInfo P90 latency >1.8s recurring) show correlated volatility across Dynamics, Compass, and Journey squads—suggesting shared infrastructure or dependency contention affecting Omni Home experience.
- ⚠️ **External blocker drag:** OMNI-1191 has been blocked 98+ days waiting on Meta; this is the third month of external dependency delays without clear resolution path. Need formal escalation protocol for ≥30-day external blockers to avoid revenue leakage.
- ⚠️ **UAT/Production gap:** BCRS UAT failures (Finance + Pre-order) indicate testing environment doesn't fully replicate production POS behavior; require enhanced staging parity before future releases.
✅ **Team responsiveness:** Nikhil Grover actively following up on OMNI blocker but lacks authority to unblock Meta; consider assigning dedicated external liaison for vendor escalations." } }

thoughts:
All three data sources have been collected and read:
  - Jira: 6 tickets (OMNI-1191 blocked, DPD-715/733 done/in release queue, DPD-734 to do, DPD-644/645 done, OPCO-1940 on hold)
  - GChat: 4 alert spaces (#dd-dpd-engage-alert, Shopping Cart Notification, #dd-dpd-grocery-alert, BCRS-UAT) with critical system instability
  - Gmail: 7 threads (calendar no events, Axelera AI promo, RMN PRD from Nikhil, Advertima TTD success, BCRS War Room meeting, Project Light meetings)

Now I need to perform Phase 3 Intelligent Analysis:
1. Group items into work streams (BCRS/OMNI/RMN/Advertima/Dynamics/Grocery/Personal)
2. Separate signal from noise (identify actual action items vs informational updates)
3. Identify cross-source correlations
4. Check for implied actions and assign urgency
5. Write executive summary, then all 4 sections
6. Save report to workdir

Work Streams identified:
  - BCRS/OMNI: OMNI-1191 (Meta blocked), OPCO-1940 (VIP logic on hold), DPD-710 epic tickets
  - Dynamics/Grocery: System flickering alerts, fp-search-indexer P2 error
  - RMN/RMN PRD: Nikhil's integration planning email
  - Advertima/TTD: Lab test success, strategic pivot discussion
  - Personal Meetings: BCRS War Room (already passed), Project Light (accepted)

Priority Assessment:
  - 🔴 Critical: fp-search-indexer P2 error (active since Mar 18), Shopping Cart Error Budget 94.8% consumed, OMNI-1191 blocked (Revenue at risk)
  - 🟡 Important: DPD-715/733 UAT complete, Omni Home discrepancy, OPCO-1940 business alignment needed
  - 🟢 Nice-to-have: Axelera AI contest, RMN PRD review, Advertima strategic discussion

On the delivery front, **OMNI-1191 (Meta offsite ads integration) remains blocked** pending Meta's campaign whitelisting response, putting $600k RMN revenue at risk with a 4-6 week development cycle looming if unblocked this quarter. Meanwhile, the BCRS UAT sign-off is delayed due to Finance and Pre-order test failures (15/15 cases failed), requiring S&G team intervention before any release can proceed. **My Action Items:** | Metadata | Details | Suggested Action | |----------|---------|-----------------| | **Priority:** 🔴

Shopping Cart Notification channel reports 94.8% error budget consumed for wishlist operations with recurring latency spikes and success rate drops.
- **Key people:** DPD Grocery Discovery On-call, dpd-pricing-cart team, Product Data Management On-call - **Open questions or risks:** (1) Root cause of fp-search-indexer errors unknown; (2) Wishlist latency correlation with checkout failures not established; (3) Error budget breach imminent if not resolved within 24 hours. - **Recommendation:** Treat as P0 incident; page on-calls immediately; initiate runbook investigations; escalate to VP if no progress by EOD tomorrow. ### RMN/RMN PRD - [Status: On Track] - **What's happening:** Nikhil Grover shared RMN PRD and requested integration planning meeting. Transition to live operations active. Strategic pivot discussed: next phase integrates visual-spatial intelligence for deterministic shopper IDs with probabilistic segments.
- **Key people:** Iman Nahvi (Advertima), Yian Koh (TTD), Ravi Singh - **Open questions or risks:** None identified; test completion milestone achieved. - **Recommendation:** Monitor live campaigns as they go "live"; stand by for implementation changes if needed. ### Personal Meetings - [Status: Completed/Passed] - **What's happening:** BCRS Refunds War Room meeting occurred March 19, 3:30–4:30 PM (already passed). Project Light meeting accepted for March 19, 4–5 PM (also passed). No upcoming meetings identified in reporting period.
- **Key people:** Prajney Sribhashyam, Alvin Choo - **Open questions or risks:** None. - **Recommendation:** Review meeting notes if available; no action required. **Patterns and Observations:** - ⚠️ **Systemic instability pattern:** `frontend-gateway` (Orchid latency P99 spikes to ~20s) and `engage-my-persona-api-go` (MyInfo P90 latency >1.8s recurring) show correlated volatility across Dynamics, Compass, and Journey squads—suggesting shared infrastructure or dependency contention affecting Omni Home experience.
- ⚠️ **External blocker drag:** OMNI-1191 has been blocked 98+ days waiting on Meta; this is the third month of external dependency delays without clear resolution path. Need formal escalation protocol for ≥30-day external blockers to avoid revenue leakage.
- ⚠️ **UAT/Production gap:** BCRS UAT failures (Finance + Pre-order) indicate testing environment doesn't fully replicate production POS behavior; require enhanced staging parity before future releases.
✅ **Team responsiveness:** Nikhil Grover actively following up on OMNI blocker but lacks authority to unblock Meta; consider assigning dedicated external liaison for vendor escalations.
